description: A hand-rolled Traefik router (raw labels, not the labels() macro) missing rate-limit@file must be flagged.
input:
Review these Traefik labels on a new externally-routed service:
```
# ansible/roles/containers/webhook/templates/docker-compose.yml.j2
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.webhook.rule=Host(`webhook.example.com`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.webhook.entrypoints=https"
      - "traefik.http.routers.webhook.tls.certresolver=cloudflare"
      - "traefik.http.routers.webhook.middlewares=crowdsec@file"
      - "traefik.http.services.webhook.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"
```
These labels are hand-written, not generated by the shared labels() macro.
rubric: PASS only if the review flags that this hand-rolled router omits the rate-limit@file middleware (the shared labels() macro adds it automatically; hand-rolled routers must add it explicitly) and recommends adding it to the middlewares chain.
